channel.title: math.HO updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.HO updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Integral-Differential Calculus
item.description: arXiv:2604.19074v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We give an exposition of the Newton-Leibniz calculus. We begin by defining the integral as a limit of Riemann sums, verify the integrals of the standard catalog of functions by direct manipulation, prove the substitution lemmas as theorems about Riemann sums, cross the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus, and harvest the differential calculus on the other side.
item.dc:creator: Grant Molnar

item.title: Development of Testing Methodology in Mathematics Education in the Context of Digitalization
item.description: arXiv:2512.18418v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Access to quality education remains a global challenge, particularly in crisis-affected regions. This study examines the decline in students' mathematical proficiency and proposes an innovative Moodle-based testing system that incorporates step-by-step solution verification and interactive exercises. Unlike traditional assessments, this approach ensures a more structured evaluation process, reducing student errors and improving feedback quality. Additionally, the study integrates Classical Test Theory to analyse test reliability, offering a novel perspective on the effectiveness of digital assessments. The proposed methodology improves assessment transparency and conceptual understanding, while Classical Test Theory is used to evaluate the reliability of multistage mathematical tasks.
item.dc:creator: I. V. Orlovskyi, O. A. Tymoshenko
